title: The Mad Real World

jokes.text: National Geographic's 'Third-World Girls Gone Wild!' - parody commercial combining nature documentaries with Girls Gone Wild
jokes.setup: Familiar Girls Gone Wild commercial format with Mardi Gras/Spring Break context
jokes.punchline: Combining it with National Geographic's exploitation of third-world poverty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical concept that exposes the exploitation in both formats. Sharp social commentary disguised as absurd commercial parody.

jokes.text: The temptation to lie would be too strong for me to do something like that. Not that I'm a liar, but, man, this is a movie. You gotta tell the legend.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Antwone Fisher writing his own movie
jokes.punchline: Admitting he'd embellish his own story because 'you gotta tell the legend'
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about human nature and moviemaking. Good setup for the parody that follows.

jokes.text: That is one funny-ass kid. And look at the penis on him! It's bigger than mine!
jokes.setup: Baby Dave being born in movie parody
jokes.punchline: Doctor commenting on newborn's penis size compared to his own
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that works within the absurd movie parody context. Uncomfortable but effective.

jokes.text: Do I come to your job and smack the broom out your hand?
jokes.setup: Heckler response setup in the movie parody
jokes.punchline: Classic putdown assuming heckler is a janitor
jokes.explanation: Classic heckler comeback. Well-executed but familiar territory.

jokes.text: spaghetti, spaghetti, spaghetti, spaghetti, spaghetti. 8 Mile.
jokes.setup: Parodying the rap battle from 8 Mile
jokes.punchline: Reducing Eminem's intense rap to just saying 'spaghetti' repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist reduction of Eminem's mom's spaghetti line. Became a cultural meme.

jokes.text: His skills took him to the top, and allowed him to have sex with Janet Jackson, Jennifer Lopez, a bunch of the girls from the 'Big Pimpin'' video and Halle Berry.
jokes.setup: Typical movie narrator talking about success
jokes.punchline: Success measured by increasingly specific sexual conquests
jokes.explanation: Typical male fantasy humor. Works in context but not particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: You drained my monster's balls.
jokes.setup: Talking to Halle Berry after sex scene
jokes.punchline: Crude description using 'monster's balls'
jokes.explanation: Shock value humor that's crude but fits the absurd fantasy context.

jokes.text: Hey, man, we were just talking about how much funnier you are than us. Y'all crazy, man. Can you come have dinner with us, Dave? I mean, really, please?
jokes.setup: Phone call from comedy legends
jokes.punchline: They're desperately begging for his attention and validation
jokes.explanation: Good fantasy reversal - the legends seeking validation from newcomer Dave.

jokes.text: You gonna bring Halle? 'Cause that's a fine bitch. Nah, son, that ain't no dinner thing.
jokes.setup: Pryor asking about bringing Halle to dinner
jokes.punchline: Dave dismissing her as not dinner-appropriate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dave's casual attitude toward his fantasy conquests.

jokes.text: Would you like to take a bath with my daughters? They could learn something from you, and I heard you have a 16-inch penis. 18, but who's countin'?
jokes.setup: Presidential meeting scene
jokes.punchline: Bush offering his daughters and discussing penis size
jokes.explanation: Absurd presidential interaction that escalates the fantasy to ridiculous proportions.

jokes.text: Matter of fact, he's just a gay guy in the city.
jokes.setup: Introducing Mario Cantone as the gay guy from Sex and the City
jokes.punchline: Reducing his role to just 'a gay guy in the city'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay on the show title. Good transitional humor.

jokes.text: Better, better... how the fuck do I know? Next question! It's better!
jokes.setup: Question about gay oral sex being better than female
jokes.punchline: Logical response followed by confident assertion anyway
jokes.explanation: Smart response that acknowledges the logical flaw in the question while still providing an answer.

jokes.text: I'll put a band-aid over your left cheek and make you my Nelly.
jokes.setup: Responding to being called 'fruity pants'
jokes.punchline: Threatening to recreate Nelly's distinctive band-aid look as dominance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture reference combined with sexual threat. Creative and memorable comeback.

jokes.text: Oh-ho, you tease. You big tease.
jokes.setup: Guy asking if Mario wants him
jokes.punchline: Flirtatious response treating the question as genuine invitation
jokes.explanation: Good character response that plays with the questioner's homophobic assumptions.

jokes.text: Visual gag: Mario retching and spitting in response
jokes.setup: Just finished flirting with the questioner
jokes.punchline: Immediate physical revulsion at the actual prospect
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual punchline that undercuts the flirtation with genuine disgust. Great timing.

jokes.text: You put him around six of the craziest white people you can find, and then expect him to live a normal life.
jokes.setup: Talking about black guys on Real World acting crazy
jokes.punchline: Of course they act crazy given the circumstances
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary that sets up the entire sketch premise. Intelligent observation about media representation.

jokes.text: This is the true story of six motherfuckin' strangers... bein' put in a motherfuckin' house... Mad Real World, son.
jokes.setup: Real World opening narration
jokes.punchline: Adding profanity and 'Mad Real World' title
jokes.explanation: Good parody setup that establishes the tone. The profanity and 'mad' twist work well.

jokes.text: Nah, uh, all the beds is full. You got to room with Tyree.
jokes.setup: Chad looking for a room
jokes.punchline: Being assigned to room with the most intimidating person
jokes.explanation: Sets up the power dynamic and Chad's predicament effectively.

jokes.text: I better not catch you standin' up peein'. You sit down when you pee, you understand?
jokes.setup: Tyree establishing dominance with roommate Chad
jokes.punchline: Emasculating bathroom rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - specific, emasculating, and reveals the power dynamic immediately.

jokes.text: Nighty-night, keep your butthole tight.
jokes.setup: Saying goodnight to Chad
jokes.punchline: Prison-influenced goodbye with sexual threat
jokes.explanation: Iconic line that perfectly captures Tyree's character. Became massively quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Let me try on some cotton Dockers, ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Chad trying to relate to his roommates
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at racial humor about cotton
jokes.explanation: Shows Chad's clueless attempts at racial bonding. Uncomfortable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What's the square root of this apartment?
jokes.setup: Chad trying to make conversation
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical math question about apartment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Chad's awkward attempts at conversation. Absurd but character-driven.

jokes.text: Look at me, America, I'm makin' juice. I call this, 'the Tron special.'
jokes.setup: Working at juice bar
jokes.punchline: Performing for cameras while making named drink
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how the housemates treat their job as performance.

jokes.text: get outta my face, nigga! I'm making juice, all right?
jokes.setup: Chad trying to get him to work properly
jokes.punchline: Aggressive response to basic work direction
jokes.explanation: Shows the workplace tension and different attitudes toward responsibility.

jokes.text: Why you wanna work so hard, huh?! Yeah, man, America wanna see us live, not work. Look at me, America, look how Tron is living.
jokes.setup: Chad asking them to work harder
jokes.punchline: Meta commentary about reality TV performance vs. actual work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on reality TV and how participants perform for cameras rather than live authentically.

jokes.text: Well, bring some juice back, nigga! Sell me some juice, bitch, I'm thirsty! Get me a Philly blunt, son. And a banana cognac, biatch!
jokes.setup: Chad getting kicked out for trying to work
jokes.punchline: Treating him like delivery service with increasingly absurd requests
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the power dynamic reversal. The requests get progressively more absurd.

jokes.text: No, you know what, Chad? I'm gonna stay here. Lysol and Tyree, they're just genuinely nice guys.
jokes.setup: Chad trying to get Katie to leave with him
jokes.punchline: Katie choosing to stay with obviously dangerous guys she calls 'nice'
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - audience knows exactly what's about to happen to naive Katie.

jokes.text: What you waitin' for, man? Get your fat ass out of here before I cut some bacon off your back.
jokes.setup: Tyree kicking Chad out of his own room
jokes.punchline: Threatening to cut 'bacon' off his back
jokes.explanation: Creative and menacing threat that's both funny and genuinely intimidating. Great character voice.

jokes.text: Yo, could you hold it down? We're tryin' to make love over here.
jokes.setup: Chad crying about his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Tyree asking HIM to be quiet while having sex with his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal of expectations. The audacity is both shocking and hilarious.

jokes.text: Niggas is wildin'!
jokes.setup: Tron in confessional reflecting on house dynamics
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about the chaos around him
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction to the insanity. Tron as the voice of reason is absurd.

jokes.text: Be careful if you get a sleeper hold because the next day your anus will really hurt.
jokes.setup: Chad giving advice about sleeper holds
jokes.punchline: Innocent warning that implies sexual assault
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny implication delivered with Chad's clueless innocence. Disturbing but effective.

jokes.text: Do they got weed in 'em? No!
jokes.setup: Chad's dad bringing brownies
jokes.punchline: Immediate assumption they contain drugs
jokes.explanation: Shows the cultural disconnect and assumptions. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Why you gotta look at me like that?! Like that! Like what?! Oh, look! Look, he doin' it! I'll look over here! Don't look at me!
jokes.setup: Chad's dad trying to be polite
jokes.punchline: Zondra escalating over normal eye contact
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of paranoia. Shows how normal behavior gets misinterpreted.

jokes.text: You came in the house with six wild niggas, that's what you did!
jokes.setup: Chad asking what his dad did wrong after being stabbed
jokes.punchline: Dad explaining the obvious reality of the situation
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest assessment of the situation. Dark humor that acknowledges the racial reality.

jokes.text: What is wrong with you people?! Can't you be quiet for one night?! It's 1:30 in the morning and I have to get up early to go to work, damn it!
jokes.setup: Chad finally snapping at the noise
jokes.punchline: Using 'you people' which will obviously be misinterpreted
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the 'you people' controversy. Shows Chad's complete obliviousness.

jokes.text: What the hell you mean, 'you people?!' I think we need to have a house meeting, y'all.
jokes.setup: Chad's innocent outburst
jokes.punchline: Immediately seizing on racial implications
jokes.explanation: Classic misinterpretation that escalates to house meeting. Perfectly executed racial comedy.

jokes.text: Tyree, you... you stabbed my dad! And you had sex with Katie.
jokes.setup: House meeting where Chad is being kicked out
jokes.punchline: Listing legitimate grievances in calm tone
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of serious accusations. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Wait a minute, you got that all wrong. I didn't have sex with Katie. Lysol had sex with Katie. I just filmed it.
jokes.setup: Being accused of having sex with Katie
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he only filmed someone else doing it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant defense that makes it worse. The casual admission of filming is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Correction, I had sex with Katie.
jokes.setup: Tyree's clarification about who had sex with Katie
jokes.punchline: Lysol matter-of-factly confirming
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan addition that piles on Chad's humiliation with bureaucratic precision.

jokes.text: But worse come to worse, we reserve the right to fuck you up.
jokes.setup: Discussing Chad leaving peacefully
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of violence as backup plan
jokes.explanation: Casually delivered threat that shows they're being 'reasonable' by giving him a choice.

jokes.text: Hey, Chad? I had sex with Katie too, man. Katie has some big-ass titties.
jokes.setup: Chad leaving after the house meeting
jokes.punchline: Tron adding insult to injury with gratuitous details
jokes.explanation: Perfect final humiliation. Tron can't resist twisting the knife with unnecessary details.

jokes.text: Come on, you rotten bitch, be my baby!
jokes.setup: Singing romantic song
jokes.punchline: Sudden turn to calling the woman a 'rotten bitch'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that subverts the romantic tone with sudden aggression.

jokes.text: Come over here so I can stick my chocolate in your peanut butter.
jokes.setup: Tyrone hitting on woman
jokes.punchline: Reese's commercial reference as pickup line
jokes.explanation: Classic Tyrone Biggums line combining commercial reference with sexual proposition. Character-perfect.

jokes.text: Chad, it's your moms. She say something wrong with you grandmother. She dead or something, my fault.
jokes.setup: Phone call for Chad
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning grandmother's death with 'my fault' disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that captures Tyree's casual cruelty. The 'my fault' makes it even worse.

tmdb_overview: Dave envisions what "The Real World" would be like with a black cast that includes one super white man. Also featuring musical performer David Broom.
